title: matelea stenantha
intro: Matelea stenantha, also known as the "climbing milk vine," is a plant species in the family Apocynaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Matelea stenantha include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of pain and infection. Herbal preparations of Matelea stenantha can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and aid in the recovery from illnesses.
